cards.title: Apple's AI Strategy Proving Smart Long-Term
cards.body: Apple is addressing criticism about losing the AI race with significant improvements to its intelligence features. The company demonstrated strategic AI integration across Siri, Photos, and other core apps at WWDC 2026.

cards.title: Sequoia Under Fire for Dual-Pricing Tactics
cards.body: Mercor's Brendan Foody publicly accused Sequoia of selling the same equity at different prices. The practice highlights ongoing valuation concerns among top venture firms.

cards.title: Sam Altman's Identity Firm Faces Layoffs
cards.body: Tools for Humanity, Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly downsizing amid revenue struggles. The layoffs come as OpenAI files for IPO.

cards.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO
cards.body: OpenAI submitted a confidential IPO filing just over a week after rival Anthropic did the same. The dual filings intensify competition between the two AI giants.

cards.title: Apple Prioritizes Software Improvements at WWDC
cards.body: Apple's WWDC keynote focused heavily on fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features alongside AI announcements. The company positioned AI as one component of broader software enhancements.

cards.title: Apple Waives Cloud API Costs for Small Devs
cards.body: As AI experimentation costs rise, Apple is removing cloud API fees for developers with under 2 million first-time App Store downloads. The move aims to democratize AI development.

cards.title: Siri Gets Dedicated App and Major Overhaul
cards.body: Apple unveiled a redesigned Siri as a standalone app with enhanced AI capabilities. The assistant now functions as a true AI companion rather than a simple voice controller.

cards.title: Apple's AI-Powered Shortcuts Accepts Natural Language
cards.body: The Shortcuts app now allows users to describe desired workflows in plain language instead of building them manually. Apple's AI interprets prompts and creates workflows automatically.

cards.title: Image Playground Gets Major Competitive Upgrade
cards.body: Apple's AI image generator received a significant makeover to increase competitiveness with other solutions. The improvements address previous limitations of the tool.

cards.title: Photos App Gains New AI Editing Features
cards.body: A new spatial "Reframe" feature lets users leverage AI to adjust photo perspectives automatically. The tool enhances editing capabilities directly within Photos.

cards.title: Siri Camera Feature Splits Bills Intelligently
cards.body: Users can now point their iPhone at a bill and use Siri to identify their items and split costs via Apple Cash. The feature simplifies expense sharing among groups.

cards.title: Safari and Password App Get AI Upgrades
cards.body: Apple added AI-powered features to Safari and its Password app to enhance functionality. The updates continue Apple's strategy of embedding AI across core applications.

cards.title: Amazon Launches AI Merchandise Design Tool
cards.body: Amazon Shopping app now lets users generate custom designs with Alexa for products like T-shirts and hoodies. Designs can be printed directly on merchandise through the app.

cards.title: AI Price Hikes Likely as Firms Go Public
cards.body: Industry observers expect token price increases as major AI companies prepare for IPOs. The race to profitability may pressure pricing across AI services.

cards.title: Notion Restores Anthropic Access After Disruption
cards.body: Notion regained access to Anthropic's services following a service disruption. The incident drew significant social media attention regarding platform dependencies.

cards.title: OpenAI Developing Super App Beyond Chat
cards.body: Senior OpenAI employees indicate the company is moving beyond chat-based interfaces. The Super App project signals a shift toward broader AI platform capabilities.

cards.title: Why Apple's slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look smart
cards.body: Apple's new AI capabilities are positioning the company competitively in the industry race. The company is demonstrating that a measured approach to AI integration can yield meaningful results.

cards.title: OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman's company does layoffs
cards.body: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling with revenue generation. The company will downsize its staff amid challenges in the market.

cards.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
cards.body: OpenAI's IPO filing comes just over a week after rival Anthropic filed to go public. The move signals an intensifying race between the two major AI firms.

cards.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
cards.body: Apple unveiled an upgraded AI-powered Siri alongside performance improvements and long-requested features. The company positioned AI as one part of a broader software improvement effort.

cards.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
cards.body: Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million App Store downloads. The move aims to lower the barrier to AI experimentation as costs rise.

cards.title: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence
cards.body: Apple made the case for an improved Siri assistant with a substantial AI component at WWDC. The overhaul demonstrates the company's commitment to integrating AI throughout its OS ecosystem.

cards.title: Apple taught your iPhone to finish sentences and workflows
cards.body: Apple is adding AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps. The updates aim to enhance user productivity with intelligent automation.

cards.title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in Shortcuts
cards.body: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade allowing users to describe desired workflows in natural language prompts. The feature simplifies workflow creation for non-technical users.

cards.title: Apple's Image Playground doesn't suck anymore
cards.body: Apple's AI image generator is undergoing a makeover to improve competitiveness. The improvements signal Apple's commitment to advancing its generative AI capabilities.

cards.title: Apple's Photos app is getting new AI editing features
cards.body: A new spatial 'Reframe' feature will let users use AI to adjust photo perspectives. The tool leverages machine learning to provide intelligent photo editing.

cards.title: Apple gives Siri its own dedicated app
cards.body: Siri is finally getting a standalone app presence. The move reflects Apple's increasing focus on AI-powered assistants as a core feature.

cards.title: Apple is fixing bill splitting with new Siri Camera feature
cards.body: Users can point their iPhone at a bill and select what they ordered to split costs via Apple Cash. The feature combines computer vision with AI processing.

cards.title: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here
cards.body: The new 'Siri AI' transforms the assistant from voice control to a full AI companion. The upgrade enables significantly more capabilities than the previous version.

cards.title: Amazon now lets you design custom merch using AI
cards.body: A new Amazon Shopping app feature allows users to generate designs with Alexa. Designs can be printed on T-shirts, hoodies, tumblers, and other products.

cards.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
cards.body: Price increases are expected as major AI companies prepare to go public. The move could significantly impact costs for AI service users.

cards.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after disruption
cards.body: Notion's head of product expressed surprise at the social media response to a service disruption. The outage affected Anthropic integrations on the platform.

cards.title: OpenAI is still working on that 'super app'
cards.body: A senior OpenAI employee declared that 'Chat is dead.' The company is pursuing a broader application beyond ChatGPT.

cards.title: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
cards.body: Psychologist Gloria Mark, who has studied digital technology interaction for 30 years, discusses how AI chatbots may impact cognitive control. The research explores the intersection of AI tools and human cognition.

cards.title: Meta hack shows more to AI security than Mythos
cards.body: Attackers used Meta's AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts by requesting account linking. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in AI systems handling authentication.

cards.title: How courts are coping with flood of AI-generated lawsuits
cards.body: Judge Maritza Braswell in Colorado reports an influx of AI-generated legal documents from unrepresented litigants. The trend reflects increased accessibility to AI writing tools for legal purposes.

cards.title: How virtual power plants could power data centers
cards.body: Google signed a deal with Voltus to support a virtual power plant in the US's largest power grid. The partnership addresses energy demands for data center operations.

cards.title: How small businesses can leverage AI
cards.body: AI capabilities span accounting, design, market research, and product development for small businesses. The technology offers significant opportunities for operational efficiency.

cards.title: Say hi to Siri AI—Apple's new conversational voice assistant
cards.body: Apple announced a reimagined Siri with conversational capabilities and expanded functionality. The updated assistant represents a significant evolution from previous versions.

cards.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
cards.body: Google integrated its latest AI models into NotebookLM for enhanced functionality. The update brings cutting-edge generative AI capabilities to the research tool.

cards.title: Chat is dead: OpenAI preps overhaul of ChatGPT
cards.body: OpenAI is preparing significant changes to ChatGPT's structure and capabilities. The overhaul suggests a shift away from chat-focused interfaces toward broader applications.

cards.title: The weather and climate science AI revolution isn't revolutionary
cards.body: Recent advances in AI for weather and climate prediction have limitations that affect their impact. The technology shows promise but isn't delivering transformative breakthroughs as expected.

cards.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm
cards.body: A survivor filed suit after an AI weapon detection system failed to identify a firearm. The case raises questions about AI reliability in critical safety applications.
